new_classes.definition_en: A spirit, other than single malt Scotch whisky or blended malt Scotch whisky, produced at a single distillery by distilling a fermented mash of malted barley with or without whole grains of other cereals; classified under CN subheadings 2208 30 61 (containers ≤ 2 L) and 2208 30 69 (containers > 2 L).

new_classes.label_en: Single Grain Scotch Whisky in Containers of More Than 2 Litres (CN 22083069)
new_classes.label_de: Single-Grain-Scotch-Whisky in Behältnissen mit mehr als 2 Litern (KN 22083069)
new_classes.definition_en: Single grain Scotch whisky produced at a single distillery from a fermented mash of malted barley with or without whole grains of other cereals, presented in containers holding more than 2 litres; classified under CN code 22083069.

new_classes.label_en: Mixed Grain Mash Basis (Malted Barley with Other Cereals)
new_classes.definition_en: A quality characterising spirits produced from a fermented mash of malted barley with or without whole grains of other cereals, as opposed to 100% malted barley mash (single malt) or a blend of whiskies from multiple malt distilleries (blended malt); used to discriminate single grain and blended grain whisky in CN 2208 30.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the key discriminating criteria for CN 22083069: (1) single distillery origin (SingleDistilleryOrigin), (2) Scotland geographic origin (ScotlandDistillationOrigin), (3) mixed grain mash basis (malted barley ± other cereals, via new MixedGrainMashBasis quality class), (4) container volume > 2 L (decimalRange restriction on maxContainerVolumeL), and (5) the CN code string 22083069. A minor gap remains: the legal text notes that single grain whisky is distinct from both single malt and blended malt Scotch whisky — this disjointness with SingleMaltWhisky and BlendedMaltWhisky is implicit through the mash-basis quality but not formally expressed as owl:disjointWith axioms (which would require complement restrictions). Additionally, the full definition of 'blended grain whisky' (a blend of grain whiskies from more than one distillery) at the parent level is not covered here since 22083069 focuses on single grain in large containers. Coverage is approximately 82% complete.
